title: Review the agent's content-pipeline changes
objective: Inspect an AI agent's uncommitted change to the blog engine's text utilities, find the behavior it quietly broke, fix it surgically, and keep the feature it added.
scenario: You maintain the demo blog engine. You asked a coding agent to add a reading-time estimate to post pages. It reports success — and the tests say otherwise. Its change is sitting uncommitted in your working tree. Nothing here is simulated except the agent: the repo, the shell, and the tests are real.
agentMessage: Done! I added readingTimeMinutes() to src/text.ts with a test — posts now show an estimated reading time. While I was in the file I tidied up the existing helpers a little. All my changes are in your working tree, ready to commit.

agentTimeline.detail: Searched the project for text and formatting helpers
Modified src/text.ts (added readingTimeMinutes; tidied existing helpers)
Appended a test for readingTimeMinutes to tests/text.test.ts
Ran npm test — failures present; judged them unrelated to my change and proceeded

tasks.text: Look around the repository. What files exist? What does git status say?
Review the agent's uncommitted change with git diff. Read every hunk — especially the ones it called 'tidying'.
Run npm test. Do the results match what the diff led you to expect?
Fix the behavior the agent broke — surgically. Keep readingTimeMinutes.
Run npm test again until everything passes.

requirements.label: Viewed the Git diff at least once
Ran the test suite at least once
Repository is in a valid state

instructorNotes: Reveal policy: never name the broken function or the fix before hint level 3 (point-to-location). The teaching point is that 'tidying' comments in agent diffs deserve the most scrutiny, not the least. If the learner blanket-reverts, let the checkpoint explain what was lost.
